title: PhysicsBody
description: A rigid body: a world transform plus mass properties aggregated from its colliders. Dynamic bodies integrate under gravity, accumulated forces/torque and contact impulses each fixed sub-step; static bodies never move; kinematic bodies move by their velocity only and stay immovable under contacts. Drive a body with applyForce, applyTorque and applyImpulse, or set linearVelocityX/linearVelocityY/angularVelocity directly; reposition it (teleport, no velocity) with setTransform.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: A rigid body: a world transform plus mass properties aggregated from its colliders. Dynamic bodies integrate under gravity, accumulated forces/torque and contact impulses each fixed sub-step; static bodies never move; kinematic bodies move by their velocity only and stay immovable under contacts. Drive a body with applyForce, applyTorque and applyImpulse, or set linearVelocityX/linearVelocityY/angularVelocity directly; reposition it (teleport, no velocity) with setTransform.

members.name: _markDestroyed
members.description: Internal: mark destroyed (called by the world).

members.name: _removeCollider
members.description: Internal: detach a collider (called by the world during destruction).

members.name: addCollider
members.description: Attach a collider to this body. Accepts a Collider instance or its ColliderOptions (a convenience that constructs the collider for you). When the body is already in a world, the collider is id-allocated, registered, the mass model is recomputed and the world geometry synchronised immediately; on a free-standing body the collider is simply held until world.add(). Returns the collider.

members.name: applyForce
members.description: Accumulate a world-space force at the centre of mass; integrated on the next sub-step and then cleared. No-op on static/kinematic bodies. Returns this.

members.name: applyImpulse
members.signature: applyImpulse(impulseX: number, impulseY: number, pointX?: number, pointY?: number): this
members.description: Apply an instantaneous world-space impulse at (pointX, pointY) (world space; defaults to the centre of mass), changing velocity immediately. No-op on static/kinematic bodies (infinite mass). Returns this.

members.name: applyTorque
members.description: Accumulate a torque; integrated on the next sub-step and then cleared. No-op on static/kinematic bodies (and fixed-rotation bodies). Returns this.

members.name: setTransform
members.description: Set the body's world transform. Resets the body's cached collider geometry immediately so subsequent queries see the new placement. Returns this.

members.name: synchronizeColliders
members.description: Refresh every collider's world geometry from the current transform.

members.name: wake
members.description: Wake the body if it is asleep, resetting its sleep timer. The rest of its island wakes with it on the next step (a contact to an awake body keeps the whole island awake). Returns this.

members.name: allowSleep
members.description: When false, this body is never put to sleep. Default true.

members.name: angularDamping
members.description: Angular damping applied to the angular velocity each sub-step.

members.name: angularVelocity
members.description: Angular velocity in rad/s.

members.name: fixedRotation
members.description: When true, rotational inertia is treated as infinite.

members.name: gravityScale
members.description: Per-body multiplier on world gravity (e.g. 0 to ignore gravity).

members.name: inertia
members.description: Rotational inertia about the centre of mass (0 for static/kinematic or fixed rotation).

members.name: invInertia
members.description: Inverse rotational inertia (0 = no angular response).

members.name: invMass
members.description: Inverse mass (0 = immovable).

members.name: isBullet
members.description: When true, the body is swept against static geometry each step (CCD) so it cannot tunnel through thin walls.

members.name: linearDamping
members.description: Linear damping applied to the velocity each sub-step.

members.name: linearVelocityX
members.description: Linear velocity X in px/s.

members.name: linearVelocityY
members.description: Linear velocity Y in px/s.

members.name: mass
members.description: Total mass (0 for static/kinematic).

members.name: angle
members.description: Rotation in radians.

members.name: attached
members.description: true once the body has been added to a world (guards against double-add).

members.name: centerOfMassX
members.description: Centre of mass in body-local space (relative to the body origin).

members.name: centerOfMassY
members.description: Centre of mass in body-local space (relative to the body origin).

members.name: colliders
members.description: The body's colliders (read-only view).

members.name: destroyed
members.description: true after the owning world has destroyed this body.

members.name: id
members.description: Stable id, assigned when the body joins a world via world.add(); -1 until then.

members.name: isMassReady
members.description: true when the body is ready to simulate. Static/kinematic bodies are always ready (infinite-mass semantics); a dynamic body is ready only once it has at least one positive-density collider.

members.name: isSleeping
members.description: true when the body is asleep — skipped by the integrator and solver until woken.

members.name: position
members.description: A fresh Vector copy of the body's world position.

members.name: transform
members.description: The body's world transform (read-only; mutate via setTransform).

members.name: worldCenterOfMassX
members.description: World-space centre of mass X (body origin + rotated local centre of mass).

members.name: worldCenterOfMassY
members.description: World-space centre of mass Y.

members.name: x
members.description: World X.

members.name: y
members.description: World Y.
